content_hints.description: The Yawning Portal is a famous inn and tavern located in the Castle Ward of Waterdeep. Its common room features a 40-foot-diameter open well, which is actually the outer shell of a sunken stone tower descending 140 feet to the first level of Undermountain.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see a massive, 30-foot-high cavern, a maze of single-story stone buildings, many of which have partially collapsed. The air is deathly quiet, and the crumbling stone buildings loom around you.

npcs.name: Durnan
npcs.dialogue: One gold each, down and back. I will wait four hours. After that, you are on your own. Any questions? Good. I do not answer questions. Step onto the platform.
npcs.notes: Retired adventurer, owner of the Yawning Portal. Over 200 years old (kept young by magic). Has been to the bottom of Undermountain and back. Speaks in clipped sentences. Judges adventurers by their gear -- good boots matter more than good swords, he says.

hazards.name: Open well shaft (140-foot drop into Undermountain)

event_triggers: On approach to well: Durnan holds out his hand. 'Gold first. Prayers second.'
On rumor: A drunken dwarf mutters about hearing his grandfather's forge hammer echoing from Level 6 -- impossible, since his grandfather died three centuries ago.

content_hints.description: The descent platform is a wooden cage reinforced with iron bands, large enough for six. Durnan works the winch with the ease of long practice, and the tavern recedes above you -- candlelight shrinking to a distant star, then nothing. The shaft walls change from brick to raw stone to something older, and the temperature drops with every foot. The rope creaks. The darkness is absolute.
content_hints.read_aloud: You step onto a wooden platform reinforced with iron bands, large enough for six if you stand close. The winch groans and the platform drops. Above you, the tavern's warm light shrinks to a coin, then a pinprick, then nothing. The shaft walls change around you -- mortared brick giving way to rough stone, then to something smoother and far older. The temperature falls steadily, and the only sounds are the creak of rope and the slow drip of water from stones that have been weeping for centuries.
event_triggers: On descent: The sounds of the tavern fade. For thirty seconds, there is only the creak of rope and the drip of water. Then the platform hits stone with a hollow boom.

content_hints.description: The landing chamber is a stone landing with no railings, leading to a door in area K7 and a stone spiral staircase descending to area K4. An invisible staircase hugs the wall as it climbs from the landing to area K11.
content_hints.read_aloud: > You see the platform settle onto a floor of polished stone beneath a domed ceiling. Everburning torches gutter in iron brackets, casting steady amber light across walls covered in graffiti. Three passages lead deeper into darkness, each marked with faded paint above the lintel.

loot.name: Adventurer's Journal (abandoned, last entry reads 'Level 3. Something following us. It has too many arms.')

event_triggers: On entry: A faint vibration in the stone -- rhythmic, like breathing. The mountain is alive.

content_hints.description: A stone arch is embedded in the wall at the west end of a 20-foot-high ledge, serving as one of Halaster's gates. This arch is composed of one hundred stone jigsaw pieces, each weighing 10 pounds.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see covered in dirt and bat guano. The air is thick with the stench of burned meat and bread, and a magical light illuminates a kitchen where a haggard male halfling frantically tries to cook, knead dough, simmer sauce, and mix spices all at once.

npcs.name: Tasselgryn 'Tass' Velldarn
npcs.dialogue: Torch? Two gold. Rope? Five gold. Healing potion? Twenty gold. You think that is expensive? Try buying one on Level 8 when you are bleeding out. Suddenly my prices look very reasonable.
npcs.notes: Halfling merchant. Fearless entrepreneur. Has been robbed seven times and always comes back. Keeps a loaded crossbow under the table and a Ring of Invisibility for emergencies. Knows more about the first three levels than most adventurers who have explored them.

content_hints.description: A secret door in the west wall opens into area 3, and north of the room is a hidden pit trap.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see a secret door in the west wall opening into area 3, and north of the room, a hidden pit trap awaits. The air here feels heavy, and you sense a hidden danger beneath the floor.

loot.name: Offering Coins (3 gp in copper and silver)

hazards.name: Scrying sensor (Halaster observes through the statue)

event_triggers: On Detect Magic: The statue's eyes flare briefly. A voice echoes from nowhere: 'How polite of you to announce yourselves.' Then silence.
On offering left: The statue's hand shifts slightly, as if accepting the gift. Perception DC 18 to notice.
